observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adhere to the following measures:
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to trace and correct the erroneous accounting entries made relative to the advance collections on RPT – Basic & SEF both for the period under audit and prior years and submit the corresponding adjusting journal entries to the Office of the Auditor with sufficient documentation; and
Henceforth, the MTO and the MAccO shall ensure that advance collections of Real Property Tax and Special Education Fund Tax are recorded in their appropriate accounts following the prescribed accounting entries, as shown in the NGAS Manual.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
1) The Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office shall coordinate with each other to the end that the former is given a copy of the loan documents sufficient enough for it to disclose the necessary information of the Loans Payable account in the Notes to Financial Statements which should henceforth be practiced; and
2) Submit to this office the schedule of Loans Payable - Domestic showing the following details as of December 31, 2024: name of the creditor, principal amount, terms of the loan, purpose of the loan and the loan status.

We recommend to the management to adopt the following measures:
Observation (a)
1) The Municipal Accounting Office personnel in-charge of recording withholding taxes and processing remittances shall endeavor to prepare a reconciliation schedule with supporting documents for the variances noted under Table 1 of this AAR in order to have a basis for an updated and reconciled balance.
Thereafter, should amounts of under remittances be determined after the year-end reconciliation, remit the same as soon as possible to avoid further penalties and possible legal issues.
Observation (b)
2) The concerned personnel in charge of the TF, SEF, 20% LDF and 5% RF books shall see to it that the amounts under Due to BIR are to be remitted on a monthly basis and prior to the scheduled remittance by the General Fund of BIR Withholding Taxes in order to avoid the utilization of cash under the General Fund to pay for BIR remittances covering other funds with no cash transferred yet. Additionally, the concerned personnel should ensure that the amount to be transferred on a monthly basis shall be exactly the amount of deductions for that specific month;
Observation (c)
3) Stop the practice of using Intra-Agency accounts under one fund only and consider the suggested Journal Entries shown in Table 3 of this AAR, in order to avoid further non-reconciliation of the Due from Other Fund account.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
Observation (a)
1) The Municipal Accounting Office personnel in-charge of recording withholding taxes and processing remittances shall endeavor to prepare a reconciliation schedule with supporting documents for the variances noted under Table 1 of this AAR in order to have a basis for an updated and reconciled balance.
Thereafter, should amounts of under-remittances be determined after the year-end reconciliation, remit the same as soon as possible to avoid further penalties and possible legal issues. Moreover, initiate a coordination meeting between the MAccO and HRMO to reconcile the variances and schedule a monthly reconciliation of said account to avoid the same circumstance to occur in the future.
Observation (b) and (c)
Direct the concerned offices to exercise diligence to ensure that withholding of tax are done accurately and remitted promptly to the BIR and to strictly adhere to the pertinent BIR Issuances to avoid penalties and surcharges, in addition to the tax thereon, that may become a personal liability of the persons responsible;
Observation (d)
Henceforth, refrain from deducting additional tax payables on compensation from CNA Incentives or Other Personnel Benefits without recognizing as Receivable the corresponding amounts

We recommended to the management to adhere to the following measures:
Observation (a)
1) Secure a copy of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2016, be knowledgeable of the procedures stated therein and among others, consistently adhere to the prescribed rates of Hazard Pay to PHWs under Item 3.3 thereof and likewise comply with the provisions of Item 3.6 on the non-entitlement to Hazard Pay of PHWs who have a combined leave and/or full time attendance in training, seminars et al. totaling at least eleven (11) working days in a month, otherwise, any excess Hazard Pay shall be subject to suspension or disallowance, as the case may be;
Observation (b)
2) Secure a copy of DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2018, be knowledgeable of the procedures stated therein and among others, consistently adhere to the prescribed formula in computing Hazard Pay for PSWs under Item 5.3.1 thereof and likewise comply with the provisions of Item 5.3.2 on the non-entitlement to Hazard Pay of PSWs for those days which they are on  leave of absence and/or attending training, seminars, et al., otherwise, any excess Hazard Pay shall be subject to suspension or disallowance, as the case may be;
Observation (c)
3) Henceforth, adhere to the documentary requirements required in Item 5.8 of COA Circular 2012-001 for the payment of Hazard Pay in order to readily facilitate the audit and validation on the grant of same and in order to guide the personnel in-charge in computing the proper amount of said benefit; and
4) Likewise, consistently attach a copy of Travel Orders and Certificates of Appearance for working days which are utilized for Official Travel in order for the concerned offices to verify the nature of the said travel which shall be a factor in determining the amount of Hazard Pay due.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
Observation (a)
1) Immediately prepare a cashbook for Direct to Cashbooks collections and record transactions pertaining thereto, including prior year transactions;
2) Assign a specific bonded personnel to accept DTC collections and name a substitute in case of his/her absence. Moreover, in case the assigned personnel ceases to function as such, ensure that the transfer of the remaining Accountable Forms to  his/her replacement shall be recorded in the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms;
3) Earmark Accountable Forms intended only for DTC and other Trust Fund collections and ensure that they are issued in numerical sequence;
Observation (b)
4) Refrain from using the functions of unbonded sub-collectors as this constitutes non-compliance with the Local Treasury Operations Manual and other pertinent rules and regulations. More importantly, it must be emphasized that unbonded sub-collectors have no accountability and that all issuance of accountable forms, collections and deposits and related shortages shall be for the account of the bonded accountable officer being represented by the said sub-collector; and
Observation (c)
5) See to it that serial numbers of the Accountable Forms (AF) issued  especially those pertaining to cash tickets are indicated in the cashbook and RCD. Accountable forms           with 50 pieces and 1,000 pieces per stub may be recorded as follows:
-AF with 50 pcs. per stub: 1234501-550 (Complete serial no. of the first issuance for the day from a stub - Last three digits of the last issuance from the same stub)
-AF with 1,000 pcs. per stub: 23497001-8000 (Complete serial no. of the first issuance
for the day from a stub - Last four digits of the last issuance from the same stub).

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
1) The Municipal Accounting Office shall immediately establish a separate subsidiary ledger for each beneficiary barangay and project(s) to be implemented under the NTF-ELCAC funds received in CY 2024 since the NADAI which includes the list of beneficiary barangays and the corresponding projects have already been received by the Municipality;
2) Direct the MTO and the MAccO to immediately process the transfer of the amount of P60,000,000.00 received under the NADAI with ADA No. RO10-ALGU-9912024063 to the Trust Fund Account of the Municipality and thereafter confirm the same with the DILG-RO, through the DILG Municipal Office (MO); and
3) Henceforth, consistently adhere with the provisions of LBC No. 154 in the implementation of projects funded under the NTF-ELCAC to ensure an effective and compliant implementation of the projects thereunder.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

1) Submit immediately the documents that were not yet submitted to the Office of the Auditor as the non-submission of the same on a untimely manner may warrant audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be;

2) Expedite the necessary steps to shift from manual to Electronic Accounting System to facilitate fast recording of transactions and timely submission of reports and other documents; and 

3) Increase manpower in the Municipal Accounting Office as lack of accounting personnel has always been the mentioned cause of the delay of submission.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

1) Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect the necessary adjustments in order to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data;

2) Henceforth, consistently see to it that transactions affecting the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds are closely monitored and that the said transactions are recorded simultaneously in the respective funds and accounts to avoid errors of omission; and

3) As much as practicable, settle immediately transactions between funds to minimize the quantity of items to be reconciled and moreover, to avoid erroneous account balances.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation by the bookkeeping section of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adhere to the following measures: 

1) With regard to the use of the account Other Payables to record business tax deducted from payments to suppliers under the Trust Fund, the account Due to Other Funds with a Subsidiary Ledger for Business Tax is suggested to be used instead of Other Payables. See sample Journal Entry below;

Trust Fund - Payment to Suppliers

Account Title	Debit	Credit

Due to NGAs	5,000,000.00	
Other Payables – Recoupment 750,000.00
Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable –
Retention			      500,000.00
Due to Other Funds [Business Tax]		50,000.00
Cash in Bank	         3,700,000.00

At the same time, a corresponding Journal Entry in the General Fund is to be made to record the equivalent amount of the recorded business tax in the Trust Fund. In this manner, Business Tax can be recorded in the proper period where it has been earned. See sample below:

General Fund - Recognition of Corresponding Business Tax

Account Title	Debit	Credit

Due from Other Funds	50,000.00	
Business Tax		50,000.00

Upon the transfer of Cash from the Trust Fund to the General Fund, the following Journal Entries are suggested to be followed:

Trust Fund - Transfer of Cash to the General Fund

Account Title	Debit	Credit

Due to Other Funds	50,000.00	
Cash in Bank		50,000.00

General Fund - Receipt of Cash from the Trust Fund
Account Title	Debit	Credit

Cash in Bank	50,000.00	
Due from Other Funds   50,000.00

2) Ensure that the transfer of cash arising from business taxes from the Trust Fund and Other Funds to the General Fund shall be made regularly and if possible, within the month when the business taxes were deducted. This is to avoid misappropriation and misuse of the amounts pertaining to business tax; and

3) Discontinue the practice of recording business tax in the General Fund of those amounts and balances which have already been closed to the Income and Expense Summary in the prior year.
observations.reason: The municipality is yet to henceforth implement the recommendations in the AOM.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adhere to the following measures:

1) Draw the Journal Entry Voucher to transfer the item Unexpended DRRMF (2012-2014) under the General Fund - Other Payables to the account Subsidy from Other Funds or Government Equity still under the General Fund, in order for the said amount to be placed under the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund which can then be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian;

2) Subsequently submit to this office the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher referred to in the preceding recommendation. The JEV shall be supported with the appropriate and complete documents; and

3) Consistently ensure that the unutilized amount after five years of the LDRRMF in the Special Trust shall be reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and under the proper Account Title in order for it to be effectively utilized for the purpose it was intended.

observations.reason: On-going tracing and locating of other supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

Stop the practice of encashing private checks using the collected funds because collections should be deposited intact and not meant for such practice, otherwise the same shall be considered as Disallowed Cash Items; and

2) Ensure that Accountable Officers are aware of their responsibilities as well as the prohibitions attached to their position.

observations.reason:
The 
municipality is yet to henceforth implement the recommendations in the AOM.

Tracing on-going.
Management reply for Auditor’s evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

Submit the lacking documents pertaining to the transactions listed in Appendix P of this report. If already submitted prior to the receipt of this report, kindly attach the corresponding transmittal letter; and

Ensure that disbursements are completely attached with the required supporting documents so as to avoid audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be.
observations.reason: Time extension requested for compliance due to retrieve lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

As much as possible, choose suppliers duly registered with regulatory government agencies who can issue Official Receipts; and

Stop using RERs as proof of payment for transactions with amounts of more than P1,000.00 as this can be considered irregular and may be subjected to audit suspension or disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

Submit to this office a copy of the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP and the DepEd School Budget covering CY 2022 and 2023 for the purpose of verifying the compliance of the SEF Budget to Sec. 4.1 and 5.2 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017;

Henceforth, consistently adhere with the guidelines prescribed in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017 with regard to the Planning, Budgeting and Utilization of the SEF to ensure that all expenditure items to be included in the SEF Budget shall be among those provided under the aforementioned Joint Circular and contained in the approved SIP and DEDP and further, that all items chargeable against the SEF shall be net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget for the DepEd and the ECCD Council, and that which may be funded out of the SEF; and

Ensure that only the priority and allowable disbursements are charged against the SEF so as not to deplete the funds for purposes other than for which it was created.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

Require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the “Unidentified Subsidiary Accounts” in the subsidiary ledgers of the mentioned real accounts to establish fair presentation, reliability and accuracy of the Statement of Financial Position accounts;

Implement the procedures laid out in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Accounts in order to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability; and

For receivable accounts, review the guidelines provided in COA Cir. No. 2016-005 on the Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances, and Fund Transfers of National Government Agencies (NGAs), Local Government Units (LGUs) and Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations (GOCCs) and consider applying the procedures thereof if the receivables of the agency, after analysis of each account/Subsidiary Ledger, meet the requirements and conditions for write-off.
observations.reason:
Reconciliation On-going

Still reviewing the guidelines set out in the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Accounts and COA Circular 2016-005.

Reconciliation and Aging On-going.

On-going review of Other Payables account.

Submit immediately the documents that were not yet submitted to the Office of the Auditor as the non-submission of the same on a untimely manner may warrant audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be;

Consider the possibility of increasing manpower in the Mun. Accounting Office since the lack of accounting personnel is one of the reasons stated which caused the delay in the submission of the abovementioned documents; and

Expedite the necessary steps to shift from manual to Electronic Accounting System to facilitate fast recording of transactions and timely submission of reports and other documents.

observations.recommendation:

Henceforth, comply with the procedures provided for by the related guidelines in the acquisition of Land for public purpose; and

Submit to this office at the soonest time possible the Disbursement Vouchers covering the acquisition of Land amounting to P200,000.00 as well as all other Disbursement Vouchers for the months September to December 2022 as the non-submission of the same on an untimely manner may warrant audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adhere to the following measures:

Submit the Statement of Time Elapsed and the photocopy of vouchers of previous payments, as applicable, of the abovementioned projects to facilitate the completion of the post-audit of the said transactions;

Submit a photocopy of the Subsidiary Ledger, duly certified by the Municipal Accountant, of each of the projects mentioned above; and

Henceforth, consistently adhere to the documentary requirements mandated by the aforesaid guidelines in order to fulfill the purpose for which the said documents are required and likewise to advance the effective examination of infrastructure related payments.

observations.recommendation: Consistently and strictly adhere to the rules and regulations provided for in EO No. 77 with regard to the payment and rates of expenses and allowances for official local travels to prevent the issuance of a Notice of Suspension or Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to submit justification on the unsigned Journal Voucher and to submit certification that he concurred the correctness of the entries stated therein.

Further, the Municipal Accountant must assure that the incoming Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) are approved and signed in order to ascertain the correctness and validity of the entries and to establish a strong internal control with the LGU’s accounting system. Also, to ensure that the consolidated financial statements are fairly presented.
observations.managementAction: Additional personnel were added to double check whether vouchers were signed and checked by the Municipal Accountant. We are also improving our internal control system.
observations.reason: Portion of the Recommendation No. 1 is yet to be implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to submit the lacking documentary supports of the various disbursements to the Office of the Auditor immediately for further review and validation.

Moreover, paid disbursement vouchers should be properly and completely substantiated with the essential documentary supports with complete signatory requirements in order to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit. Also, management should revisit and adhere strictly to COA Circular 2012-001 which provides the proper documentary requirements for common government transactions.

We recommended to the management to submit the supporting documents immediately for us to complete the post-audit of the said transactions. 

We further recommended to the management, particularly to the Municipal Accountant being the Internal Auditor of the LGU, to check the completeness of the supporting documents for every disbursement of government fund or before any payment can be made. This is to avoid suspension or disallowance of transactions in audit.

observations.recommendation:

1) Submit immediately the documents that were not yet submitted to the Office of the Auditor as the non-submission of the same on a untimely manner may warrant audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be;
2) Expedite the necessary steps to shift from manual to Electronic Accounting System to facilitate fast recording of transactions and timely submission of reports and other documents; and

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
1) Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect the necessary adjustments in order to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data;
Henceforth, consistently see to it that transactions affecting the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds are closely monitored and that the said transactions are recorded simultaneously in the respective funds and accounts to avoid errors of omission; and
3) As much as practicable, settle immediately transactions between funds to minimize the quantity of items to be reconciled and moreover, to avoid erroneous account balances.

Trust Fund - Payment to Suppliers
At the same time, a corresponding Journal Entry in the General Fund is to be made to record the equivalent amount of the recorded business tax in the Trust Fund. In this manner, Business Tax can be recorded in the proper period where it has been earned. See sample below:
General Fund - Recognition of Corresponding Business Tax
Upon the transfer of Cash from the Trust Fund to the General Fund, the following Journal Entries are suggested to be followed:
Trust Fund - Transfer of Cash to the General Fund
General Fund - Receipt of Cash from the Trust Fund
2) Ensure that the transfer of cash arising from business taxes from the Trust Fund and Other Funds to the General Fund shall be made regularly and if possible within the month when the business taxes were deducted. This is to avoid misappropriation and misuse of the amounts pertaining to business tax;
2) Discontinue the practice of recording business tax in the General Fund of those amounts and balances which have already been closed to the Income and Expense Summary in the prior year.

We recommended to the management to adhere to the following measures:
1) Draw the Journal Entry Voucher to transfer the item Unexpended DRRMF (2012-2014) under the General Fund - Other Payables to the account Subsidy from Other Funds or Government Equity still under the General Fund, in order for the said amount to be placed under the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund which can then be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the local sanggunian;
2) Subsequently submit to this office the corresponding Journal Entry Voucher referred to in the preceding recommendation. The JEV shall be supported with the appropriate and complete documents; and
Consistently ensure that the unutilized amount after five years of the LDRRMF in the Special Trust shall be reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and under the proper Account Title in order for it to be effectively utilized for the purpose it was intended.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
Stop the practice of encashing private checks using the collected funds because collections should be deposited intact and not meant for such practice, otherwise the same shall be considered as Disallowed Cash Items; and
2) Ensure that Accountable Officers are aware of their responsibilities as well as the prohibitions attached to their position.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
Submit the lacking documents pertaining to the transactions listed in Appendix P of this report. If already submitted prior to the receipt of this report, kindly attach the corresponding transmittal letter; and
Ensure that disbursements are completely attached with the required supporting documents so as to avoid audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
As much as possible, choose suppliers duly registered with regulatory government agencies who can issue Official Receipts; and
Stop using RERs as proof of payment for transactions with amounts of more than P1,000.00 as this can be considered irregular and may be subjected to audit suspension or disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
Submit to this office a copy of the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP and the DepEd School Budget covering CY 2022 and 2023 for the purpose of verifying the compliance of the SEF Budget to Sec. 4.1 and 5.2 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017;
Henceforth, consistently adhere with the guidelines prescribed in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017 with regard to the Planning, Budgeting and Utilization of the SEF to ensure that all expenditure items to be included in the SEF Budget shall be among those provided under the aforementioned Joint Circular and contained in the approved SIP and DEDP and further, that all items chargeable against the SEF shall be net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget for the DepEd and the ECCD Council, and that which may be funded out of the SEF; and
Ensure that only the priority and allowable disbursements are charged against the SEF so as not to deplete the funds for purposes other than for which it was created.

Implement the procedures laid out in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Accounts in order to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability; and

For receivable accounts, review the guidelines provided in COA Cir. No. 2016-005 on the Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances, and Fund Transfers of National Government Agencies (NGAs), Local Government Units (LGUs) and Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations (GOCCs) and consider applying the procedures thereof if the receivables of the agency, after analysis of each account/Subsidiary Ledger, meet the requirements and conditions for write-off.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to submit justification on the unsigned Journal Voucher and to submit certification that he concurred the correctness of the entries stated therein.

Further, the Municipal Accountant must assure that the incoming Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) are approved and signed in order to ascertain the correctness and validity of the entries and to establish a strong internal control with the LGU’s accounting system. Also, to insure that the consolidated financial statements are fairly presented.
observations.managementAction: Additional personnel were added to double check whether vouchers were signed and checked by the Municipal Accountant. We are also improving our internal control system.
observations.reason: Pending complete certification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to prudently adhere to the correct disbursement process. All disbursements of government funds shall invariably bear the approval of the proper officials, such that no check shall be issued unless all the required signatories are present.

We recommended to the management to be more prudent in exercising their fiscal responsibility by strictly adhering to the proper disbursement process and refrain from signing or countersigning blank checks. Moreover, implement a good internal control system to prevent incurrence of such improper practice of paying disbursement vouchers with inadequate signatures of proper officials.

We recommended to the management to submit the lacking documentary supports of the various disbursements to the Office of the Auditor immediately for further review and validation.

Moreover, paid disbursement vouchers should be properly and completely substantiated with the essential documentary supports with complete signatory requirements in order to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit. Also, management should revisit and adhere strictly to COA Circular 2012-001 which provides the proper documentary requirements for common government transactions.
observations.reason: Various vouchers still lacked the required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the HRMO and Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the required supporting documents on the monthly claim of salaries, RATA and PERA of the Sanggunian Bayan Members and staffs.  Moreover, require the 
submission of the lacking documentary requirements for the foregoing mentioned period.  Unless otherwise they are submitted, the condition affected the accuracy of the earned leave balances of concerned personnel.

We recommended to the management to submit the supporting documents immediately for us to complete the post-audit of the said transactions. 

We further recommended to the management, particularly to the Municipal Accountant being the Internal Auditor of the LGU, to check the completeness of the supporting documents for every disbursement of government fund or before any payment can be made. This is to avoid suspension or disallowance of transactions in audit.
observations.managementAction: The duplicate and supporting documents were attached to the JEV of the payables in December 2020. JEV Nos of the original copies were indicated in the DVs upon payment in 2021.                 For December 2021 payables we have attached some copies of the Obligation Slips and Vouchers. The other attachments were included on the payment of the said payables in 2022 as per recommendation of COA.
observations.reason: Various vouchers still lacked the required supporting documents.

observations.managementAction:
The concerned offices were only obliged to provide justification. They were also required to submit 
Attendance sheets.
observations.reason: The noted lacking documents were not completely submitted to COA auditors.
Submitted documents subject to Audit evaluation and/or action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to obtain bank statements from the concerned depository banks to reconcile the accounts and properly reflect the closed accounts in the books; and to follow up with the banks to provide the necessary bank statements to support bank reconciliation.
observations.reason: No Bank Statements were submitted yet to the Auditor’s Office to aid in the verification of the BRS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to demand Banks Statements from the Depository Banks pursuant to the above cited regulation, which are requisite in the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements. In so doing the existence of Cash in Bank Account could be ascertained
observations.reason: No demands were submitted to the Office

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accounting office to see to it that recording of similar transactions are made in accordance with IPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2015-009, and make the following adjusting entries to correct the said error:

Accounts Payable			₱ 8,956,294.95
Construction in Progress – 		₱ 8,956,294.95
Building & other structures

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to submit justification on the unsigned Journal Voucher and to submit certification that he concurred the correctness of the entries stated therein.

Further, the Municipal Accountant must assure that the incoming Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) are approved and signed in order to ascertain the correctness and validity of the entries and to establish a strong internal control with the LGU’s accounting system. Also, to insure that the consolidated financial statements are fairly presented.

We recommended to the management to prudently adhere to the correct disbursement process. All disbursements of government funds shall invariably bear the approval of the proper officials, such that no check shall be issued unless all the required signatories are present.

We recommended to the management to be more prudent in exercising their fiscal responsibility by strictly adhering to the proper disbursement process and refrain from signing or countersigning blank checks. Moreover, implement a good internal control system to prevent incurrence of such improper practice of paying disbursement vouchers with inadequate signatures of proper officials.

We recommended to the management to submit the lacking documentary supports of the various disbursements to the Office of the Auditor immediately for further review and validation.

Moreover, paid disbursement vouchers should be properly and completely substantiated with the essential documentary supports with complete signatory requirements in order to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit. Also, management should revisit and adhere strictly to COA Circular 2012-001 which provides the proper documentary requirements for common government transactions.

We recommended to the HRMO and Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the required supporting documents on the monthly claim of salaries, RATA and PERA of the Sanggunian Bayan Members and staffs.  Moreover, require the submission of the lacking documentary requirements for the foregoing mentioned period.  Unless otherwise they are submitted, the condition affected the accuracy of the earned leave balances of concerned personnel.

We recommended to the management to submit the supporting documents immediately for us to complete the post-audit of the said transactions.

We further recommended to the management, particularly to the Municipal Accountant being the Internal Auditor of the LGU, to check the completeness of the supporting documents for every disbursement of government fund or before any payment can be made. This is to avoid suspension or disallowance of transactions in audit.

We recommended to the management the following:
Advise the payroll personnel to follow the rules under Section 1, Rule VI of R.A. No. 9679 for purposes of properly computing the government share as well as personal share of the HDMF contributions.
Advice the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds to cover employer’s contribution due the HMDF based on the maximum monthly compensation of P5,000.00, otherwise the computation of allowable PS limitation will be adversely affected.
Strengthen the internal control system particularly the review of remittances on statutory obligations to prevent from recurrence of the deficiency; and,
Make representation to HMDF to have the excess government contributions be off-set against future or succeeding month’s government share contributions, if warranted.

We recommended to the management, particularly to the Municipal Accountant, to exert extra effort in facilitating the submission of the year-end Financial Statements of the Municipality of Kitaotao on or before every February 14 of the following year.

Further, we recommended to the management to require all concerned offices of the LGU to make the proper coordination to be able to prepare and submit the financial reports of the municipality within the reglementary period prescribed by law.
We recommended to the management to submit a legal basis for the grant allegedly as COVID-19 assistance, to its permanent, casual, co-terminus and job-order employees.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to trace those undetermined and unreconciled Construction in Progress balances between the General Ledger and the Subsidiary Ledger (SL) through exerting extra effort in the retrieval of the supporting schedules and documents to ascertain the balances of the account as reflected in the Financial Statements as of December 31, 2020. However, if there is no way that the Accounting Office can retrieve the records of these balances, it should follow the guidelines under COA Circular 2020-006 for the “One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies” to assist the latter in coming up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

Also, the Municipal Accountant should then maintain complete supporting records, schedules and documents to monitor future projects under the CIP account so as to eventually close the same to the asset account as appropriate.

We recommended to the management to:
•	Establish the accuracy  and  reliability  of  the  recorded  liability accounts  by  exerting effort   in   tracing and clearing   the   undetermined accounts in   order   to   provide   reliable   information to readers of the financial statements. Also, provide the amount  with  a complete schedule  and/or  subsidiary  ledger  and  the details of the items listed therein;
•	Conduct thorough review of the affected accounts to determine the cause of abnormal/negative balances and adjust the affected accounts.
•	To prepare the working papers with complete information and details needed in order to arrive to the proper amounts and correct accounting entries in effecting the adjustments related to the liability accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended to the management to follow the procedural guidelines under COA Circular 2020-006 for the “One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies” to assist the latter in coming up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to analyze the prior years’ General Ledger (GL) balances of the Receivable accounts to reconcile with the Subsidiary Ledegrs (SL). Trace the undetermined Receivable account balance between the General Ledger (GL) and the Subsidiary Ledger (SL) through exerting extra effort in the retrieval of the supporting schedules and documents and to review the details and status of the balances thereafter in order to determine the appropriate action to be done. The write-off of balances may be recommended by the Accountant if warranted   subject to prescribed procedures, rules, and regulations of COA.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry in order to record the aggregate amount of ₱ 2,982,813.84 in Due to Other Funds account in their respective books of accounts. 

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant must start to set-up Due to Other Funds on the period the taxes withheld by other funds were paid by the General Fund Proper. He must also insure that these reciprocal accounts are even and reconciled at all instances in order to ascertain that the financial statements are fairly presented.

We recommended to the management to regularly review the unexpended balance of the Disaster Risk and Management Fund at the end of the budget year and any unspent balance of the appropriation should be transferred to the Special Trust Fund under the “Trust Liabilities – DRRMF” account on the same year. 

We further recommended to the Municipal Accountant to also perform adjusting entries of the affected accounts, if warranted, together with the supporting documents and working paper and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor following the entries under Annex C of COA Circular 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended to the management to require the concerned officers and employees to determine the causes of delays in the conduct of procurement and put the same into writing and furnishing thereof a copy to the Office of the Auditor. 

We further recommended to the management, for future application, to comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR, observing the required timeline in the procurement to expedite the process and avoid penalties under Rule XXI of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended to the management to direct the BAC to require all winning bidders of the goods and services and infrastructure projects to submit Performance Securities within the prescribed period and to enforce sanctions provided in Section 40 of the IRR-A. Advise the BAC to impose upon winning bidders complete details of the securities, including issuance dates and validity periods in order to ensure that such securities guarantee faithful compliance of contractors prior to contract signing, otherwise, the LGU shall not accept performance securities which is insufficient by reason earlier cited.

We recommended to the management to strictly implement the provisions mentioned under COA Circular No. 2009-001 and direct the personnel-in-charge to render extra efforts with the release of contracts, together with all the supporting documents, to furnish to the Commission on Audit, within five (5) working days upon issuance.

We also recommended to the management that the lacking supporting documents of the contracts as enumerated under Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the goods and infrastructure projects and as specified under the list of lacking supporting documents for each contract under this report be submitted to the office to allow the team to post-audit the affected procurement activities.

Require the concerned officers and employees to comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

Require the Accountant to issue demand letter for those with long overdue cash advances and subsequently withhold payment of salaries and other emoluments due to the concerned officials and employees until after final liquidation or settlement of their cash advances shall have been made.

We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to issue Official Receipt immediately whenever there will be transfer of fund thru check to other depository account owing to other funds will be made in the subsequent periods.

We recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to return the P 2,364,950.40 from the Trust Fund-IMREMP to 20% Municipal Development Fund representing the excess transferred amount of LGU equity.  Further, we require that the concerned LGU’s key personnel must always coordinate with each other in order to avoid similar slip-up in the forthcoming years.

Moreover, the Office of the Auditor must be furnished with the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) if the refund was made thru ADA and machine copy of the Official Receipt (OR) if done thru check.

Further, we recommend to the Municipal Accountant to record any subsequent 15% mobilization fund to Advances to Contractors account instead of Other Receivables.

We recommended to the management, particularly to the Municipal Accountant, to facilitate the submission of the year-end Financial Statements of the Municipality of Kitaotao on or before every February 14 of the following year.  

Further, we recommended to the management to require all concerned offices of the LGU to make the proper coordination to be able to prepare and submit the financial reports of the municipality within the reglementary period prescribed by law.

We recommended to the management, particularly to the Municipal Treasurer, to timely prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIURP) to report all unserviceable and obsolete items previously taken up in the books of accounts as assets so that they may be properly requested for disposal to the Office of the Auditor and thereafter, to be dropped from the books of accounts of the municipality. 

Also, we recommended to the management to create specifically a disposal committee to conduct inspection/inventory of disposable assets and determine its appraised value and the mode of disposal in accordance with the existing guidelines on supply and property management.

We recommended to the management to timely remit the taxes withheld by other funds to General Fund Proper on the subsequent months, ideally 10 days following the end of the month as prescribed by law.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Treasurer to submit on time the Disbursement Vouchers, Payrolls and Official Receipts including its supporting documents to the Municipal Acounting Office in a timely manner. Thereafter, the Accounting Office, to submit without undue delay to the Office of the Auditor for verification and post-audit.

We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to effect the following adjusting entries in the ensuing year to correct the account balances of the affected accounts.

1. On expended BGCM for the year
a. Government Equity P  22,306,352.97 (debit)
Prior Years Adjustments P 22,306,352.97 (credit)

2. On unexpended BGCM for the year (must be treated as income in CY 2020)
a. Other Payables P 626,926.03 (debit)
Prior Years Adjustments P 626,926.03 (credit)

Pursuant to Section 3.9 of DBM LBC 125, funds which remain unutilized after the lifting of the State of Calamity, by Presidential Proclamation, shall be reverted to the National Treasury by the recipient cities and municipalities.

We recommended to the Management to ask for the Certified List of Beneficiaries from the DSWD so that the Audit Team could validate existence/legitimacy of the reported recipients and validity, legality and propriety of the payments could be established.

We further recommended to the management that thereafter, to observe religiously specific guidelines in implementing programs funded by national offices in order to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

However, if there is no way that the Accounting Office can retrieve the records of these balances, it should follow the guidelines under COA Circular 2020-006 for the “One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies” to assist the latter in coming up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
Also, the Municipal Accountant should then maintain complete supporting records, schedules and documents to monitor future projects under the CIP account so as to eventually close the same to the asset account as appropriate.

Government Equity 			P  22,306,352.97

Pursuant to Section 3.9 of DBM LBC 125, funds which remain unutilized after the lifting of the State of Calamity, by Presidential Proclamation, shall be reverted to the National Treasury by the recipient cities and municipalities.

We recommended to the Management to ask for the Certified List of Beneficiaries from the DSWD so that the Audit Team could validate existence/legitimacy of the reported recipients and validity, legality and propriety of the payments could be established.
We further recommended to the management that thereafter, to observe religiously specific guidelines in implementing programs funded by national offices in order to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to demand Banks Statements from the Depository Banks pursuant to the above cited regulation, which are requisite in the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements. In so doing the existence of Cash in Bank Account could be ascertained.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to set-up “Due to Other Funds” account in the GF Proper to establish liability from other funds in the event time is the essence to cope with the deadline set by the BIR and when there is still no actual cash transferred yet from other funds.
We further recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to conduct timely reconciliations on the discrepancies between the recorded amounts of Due to BIR and the remittances of the same while ensuring complete and appropriate supporting documents as bases in recording. Also, to require the Municipal Treasurer to remit promptly the balances of Due to BIR account in order not to compromise the benefits due to the intended beneficiaries and at the same time avoid penalties/surcharges arising from late remittances.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to require the concerned officers and employees to comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
We further recommended to the management to require the Accountant to issue demand letter for those with long overdue cash advances and subsequently withhold payment of salaries and other emoluments due to the concerned officials and employees until after final liquidation or settlement of their cash advances shall have been made.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to strictly implement the provisions mentioned under COA Circular No. 2009-001 and direct the personnel-in-charge to render extra efforts with the release of contracts to furnish to the Commission on Audit, within five (5) working days upon issuance.

We recommended to the management to review the budget as well as the Programs, Activities and Projects (P/A/Ps) of the Municipality to align with the requirements stated under Section 7 (b) of RA No. 10410, otherwise known as the "Early Years Act of 2013" which provides for the allocations of SEF for the ECCD Program so that the intended beneficiaries/school children can enjoy the benefits and their educational standards will be uplifted.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned LGU officials to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) on or before the 15th day of the following month in compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-002 and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Plan (LDRRMP) annually to the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) for review and evaluation.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to facilitate the submission of the CY 2019 year-end Financial Statements and require the accounting personnel to render extra efforts if warranted in order to comply with the reglementary period and coordinate with concerned offices in order to prevent unnecessary delays in the preparation and submission of these reports.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the management to submit the following documents in order to facilitate continuance of our audit and validate that the Municipality have met all the CSC Memorandum Circular requirements warranting the grant of the monetary awards:
Detailed Employee Suggestions and Incentive Awards System
Documents evidencing the generation of savings due to any extraordinary contribution or exceptional accomplishment by an employee which resulted in such monetary savings. In case savings are generated, monetary awards shall be granted which shall not exceed 20% thereof.
Approved Program on Awards and Incentives for Service Excellence (PRAISE) Model by the Civil Service Commission (CSC)

We recommended to the management to instruct the General Services Officer or the Municipal Treasurer to ensure government properties to General Insurance fund by the Government Service Insurance System and to direct the responsible personnel to prepare the pertinent reports and documents under his/her provision.

Require the Municipal Accountant to demand Banks Statements from the Depository Banks pursuant to the above cited regulation, which are requisite in the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements. In so doing the existence of Cash in Bank Account could be ascertained. Also, the accounts must be reflected in the Notes to Financial Statements disclosing the information of the two (2) Cash in Bank accounts, otherwise, actions must be taken in case the account must be written off.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Subsidiary Ledger and include in the Subsidiary of fixed Assets – Land Account, the complete description and all necessary information on each item of the acquired land.
Reconcile or Re-assess the Land Market Value submitted by the MASSO with records of the Accounting Division and make necessary adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIURP) to report all unserviceable and obsolete items previously taken up in the books as assets so that they may be properly disposed of and dropped from the accounts. In addition, Municipal Accountant should reclassify the unserviceable properties to Other Assets account and expendable supplies or property already consumed should no longer be carried in the books as inventory but should be accounted for as expense or as prior year’s adjustments.
Also, we recommended that management creates a disposal committee to conduct inspection/inventory of disposable assets and determine its appraised value and the mode of disposal in accordance with the existing guidelines on supply and property management.

Enhance the effort to reconcile the outstanding balances of Loans Payable to reflect the correct balance of loans to be presented in the Financial Statements.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize accrued interest payable at year-end and set-up subsidiary record for each loan accounts in order to enhance the monitoring of loan payments and the balances.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to follow the afore-cited provision. Regularly review the unexpended LDRRMF which are already more than five (5) years in the special trust fund. Consequently, the pertinent amounts should be reverted back to the general fund of the municipality so that it can be utilized by making it available for other social services as stipulated therein.
Facilitate the submission of the CY 2018 year-end FS.  Require the accounting personnel to render extra efforts if warranted in order to comply with the reglementary period.

Require the concerned officers and employees to comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
Require the Accountant to issue demand letter for those with long overdue cash advances and subsequently withhold payment of salaries and other emoluments due to the concerned officials and employees until after final liquidation or settlement of their cash advances shall have been made.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned LGU officials to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) on or before the 15th day of the following month in compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-002 and to properly submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Plan (LDRRMP) annually to the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) for review and evaluation.

We recommended that management strictly implement the provisions mentioned under COA Circular No. 2009-001 and direct the personnel-in-charge to render extra efforts with the release of contracts to furnish to the Commission on Audit, within five (5) working days upon issuance, the approved POs.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to insure government properties to General Insurance fund by the Government Service Insurance System and to direct the responsible personnel to prepare the pertinent reports and documents under his/her provision.

We recommended to require the Municipal Engineer and Other Officers concerned for proper prioritization of fully funded projects to ensure their completion on its target set, thus precluding unwarranted delay of the implementation of programs, projects and activities.

Require the Municipal Accountant to demand Banks Statements from the Depository Banks pursuant to the above cited regulation, which are requisite in the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements. In so doing the existence of Cash in Bank Account could be ascertained.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Subsidiary Ledger and include in the Subsidiary of fixed Assets – Land Account, the complete description and all necessary information on each item of the acquired land.
Reconcile or Re-assess the Land Market Value submitted by the MASSO with records of the Accounting Division and make necessary adjustments.
Facilitate the submission of the CY 2017 year-end FS.  Require the accounting personnel to render extra efforts if warranted in order to comply with the reglementary period.

Require the concerned officers and employees to comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
Require the Accountant to issue demand letter for those with long overdue cash advances and subsequently withhold payment of salaries and other emoluments due to the concerned officials and employees until after final liquidation or settlement of their cash advances shall have been made.
Strictly adhere to the provision of RA 9003 and establish Materials Recovery Facility to minimize the solid waste of the municipality.

We recommended to require the Municipal Budget Officer to incorporate PPAs in accordance with the approved Annual Investment (AIP)/Development Plan pursuant to afore-cited provisions.  Constructions of Multi –Purpose Building (Day Care Centers) as well as acquisition of lot for relocation site with lump sum appropriation with no specific/itemized projects as to the location, recipients to be benefited for,   are not found in the context of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.  It is suggested also that the municipality shall reprogram/revise those disqualified projects and ensure that these are aligned with the aforementioned regulation and they are covered with a Supplemental AIP duly approved by the Sangguniang Bayan indicating the itemized programs, projects and activities. Request the Technical Review Committee for consideration on disqualified project which had incurred expenditure already.  Ensure timely submission of all budgets enacted so that incurrence of expenditures for inoperative appropriation(s), if any, could be avoided.
We recommended to facilitate the submission of the LDRRMP to the Office of the Civil Defense for review and evaluation and direct the officials concerned to strictly follow R.A. No.10121.
We recommended management implement strict observance of the reglementary period in the submission of financial reports and accounts to the Auditor to give him ample time for review
observations.reason: Lack of Personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended management submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
observations.reason: Lack of Personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended management require officers and employees concerned to comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; and withhold payment of salaries and other emoluments due to the officials and employees with outstanding cash advances until after final liquidation or settlement of their cash advances shall have been made.
observations.reason: Lack of Personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended management require the accountant submits the bank reconciliation statements on time for review and verification, to ascertain the propriety, validity of cash accounts.
observations.reason: Lack of Personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended management create an Inventory Team to conduct physical count of property, plant and equipment of the agency and require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the result of the account with the accounting records and to effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: Lack of Personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended management require complete documentation in the disbursement of LGU funds so as not to cause delay in the processing and recording of the same in the accounting office; and Implement strict observance of the reglementary period in the submission of financial reports and accounts to the Auditor to give him ample time for review.
We recommended management require the accountant to submit the bank reconciliation statements on time for review and verification, to ascertain the propriety, validity of cash accounts.

We recommended management require officers and employees concerned to comply with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; and withhold payment of salaries and other emoluments due to the officials and employees with outstanding cash advances until after final liquidation or settlement of their cash advances shall have been made.

We recommended management create an Inventory Team to conduct physical count of property, plant and equipment of the agency and require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the result of the account with the accounting records and to effect the necessary adjustments.

We recommended management submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

Require complete documentation in the disbursement of LGU funds so as not to cause delay in the processing and recording of the same in the accounting office.  Implement strict observance of the reglementary period in the submission of financial reports and accounts to the Auditor to give him ample time for review.

Require officers and employees concerned to observe the prescribed liquidation period of cash advances.

Withhold payment of salaries and other emoluments due to the officials and employees with outstanding cash advances until after final liquidation or settlement of their cash advances shall have been made.
Direct the HRMO Officer to strictly adhere to the Omnibus Rules on Leave in processing of application for monetization of leave credits for the Official/employees of the Entity.
Direct all the Accountable Officers to apply for fidelity bond and the amount of bond shall be in accordance with their maximum cash accountability.
We recommend that the Disbursing Officer / Cashier stamp “PAID” all DVs and its supporting documents after payment is made to prevent the re-use of the same on similar transaction.
In order to ascertain the propriety, validity and legality of cash account, we recommend that the accountant submits the bank reconciliation statements on time for review and verification.

Require the Municipal Engineer and Other Officers concerned for proper prioritization of fully funded projects to ensure their completion on its target set, thus precluding unwarranted delay of the implementation of programs, projects and activities.
Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust CIP accounts once the Municipal Engineer submit the list of completed and on-going projects and they should tally their records.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold salaries and other emoluments of the person liable that are still in government service until the disallowance is fully settled.  Also, issue demand letter to the persons liable who are retired/separated from government service.

Direct the officials and employees concerned to submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel consumption to the auditor for review and evaluation, Also, require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare/monitor Registry of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations – Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (RAAOMO) to avoid over charging.

Accredit the NGOs/POs and execute MOA or loan agreements covering the loan releases following the guideline prescribed under COA Circular No, 2007-00 dated October 25, 2007. 

Direct the municipal accountant to reclassify/correct the erroneous entry in the books of accounts and   maintain individual subsidiary ledger to the loan account.	
Designate a Livelihood Officer to monitor the implementation and to intensify the collection of the livelihood assisted projects loans.

Create an Inventory Team to conduct physical count of property, plant and equipment of the agency and require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the result of the account with the accounting records and to effect the necessary adjustments.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the issuance of accountable forms to the collectors to deter delay in turnover of collections.
